Cártel de los Rojos.description: Mexican criminal organization that emerged as a split from the Beltrán Leyva Cartel, based in Guerrero and Morelos. Founded around 2009 by Jesús Nava Romero and others, it engaged in drug trafficking, extortion, kidnapping, and other crimes. The group was dismantled by 2022.

descriptions: Los Rojos split from the Beltrán-Leyva Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: El Cártel de los Rojos (conocidos popularmente como Los Rojos) fue una organización criminal mexicana surgida como escisión del Cartel de los Beltran Leyva

edges.evidence_quote: No se debe confundir con la célula delincuencial de los Rojos, que son una escisión del Cártel del Golfo.

Contini clan.description: The Contini clan is a powerful Neapolitan Camorra clan based in Naples, Italy, founded in the early 1980s by Edoardo Contini. It operates primarily in the Arenaccia district and Naples Central Station area, with international activities in Spain, the Netherlands, Scotland, and Eastern Europe. The clan is a founding member of the Secondigliano Alliance and is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and money laundering. It is considered one of the most powerful Camorra groups, known for its lack of internal divisions and informants.

descriptions: Giuliano and Contini fought a faida in 1984.
edges.evidence_quote: faida tra i Giuliano e i Contini : combattuta nel 1984

descriptions: The Contini clan is a subgroup of the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: The Camorra's organizational structure is divided into individual groups called 'clans'.

descriptions: Contini clan is a Camorra clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Antonio Righi, called 'Tonino il biondo', was considered by the investigators as a money laudererer on behalf of the Contini and Mazzarella clans in London.

descriptions: Contini clan is a Camorra clan, part of the Camorra organization.
edges.evidence_quote: According to Misso, the real power of the organization is now in the hands of the Licciardi clan , Mallardo clan , Moccia clan and Contini clan .

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Secondigliano Alliance Contini clan

Al-Qaeda in Iraq.description: Salafi jihadist terrorist organization founded on 17 October 2004 by Abu Musab al-Zarqawi, operating in Iraq. It was affiliated with al-Qaeda and aimed to expel U.S.-led coalition forces and establish an Islamic state. The group was disbanded on 15 October 2006 when it merged into the Islamic State of Iraq.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda established Al-Qaeda in Iraq as its regional branch.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2004, al-Qaeda launched its Iraqi regional branch.

edges.evidence_quote: Allegations of Qatari support ... Nuaimi has facilitated significant financial support to al-Qaeda in Iraq

edges.evidence_quote: Former groups: ... AQI , (2004–2006)

descriptions: Al-Qaeda forces were embedded in Jama'at Al-Tawhid wal-Jihad.
edges.evidence_quote: In Iraq, al-Qaeda forces loosely associated with the leadership were embedded in the Jama'at Al-Tawhid wal-Jihad group commanded by Abu Musab al-Zarqawi.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2004 it pledged allegiance to Osama bin Laden 's al-Qaeda.

'Ndrina Lo Bianco.description: The Lo Bianco are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Vibo Valentia, Calabria, Italy. They are allied with the Barba clan within the local 'Ndrangheta structure. Their illegal activities include public works contracts, extortion, usury, and control of the healthcare sector. The clan has been targeted by operations such as Nuova Alba (2007) and The Goodfellas (2010).

descriptions: Bonavota is allied with Lo Bianco.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bonavota are allies of the Anello, Lo Bianco and Mancuso 'ndrina.

edges.evidence_quote: I Lo Bianco sono una 'ndrina calabrese di Vibo Valentia alleata dei Barba all'interno dell'organigramma del Locale di Vibo Valentia.

descriptions: Lo Bianco were involved in the same operation targeting Mancuso, but no direct relationship is specified.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 19 dicembre 2019 a conclusione dell'operazione Rinascita-Scott contro i Mancuso e tutta la 'ndrangheta del vibonese, vengono coinvolti anche i Lo Bianco.

descriptions: Lo Bianco expanded their criminal activities at the expense of Pardea, indicating rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: I Lo Bianco riescono ad espandersi con le loro attività criminali a discapito dei Pardea, la prima 'ndrina nata a Vibo Valentia.

descriptions: Al-Mujamma' al-Islami was created by a Muslim Brotherhood figure and encapsulated religious organizations.
edges.evidence_quote: The creation of al-Mujamma' al-Islami, the Islamic Center in 1973 by Shaykh Ahmad Yasin had a centralizing effect that encapsulated all religious organizations.

Spanish Cobras.description: Primarily Latino street gang based in Chicago, Illinois, founded in the 1970s by former members of the Maniac Latin Disciples. Active in multiple Midwestern states and parts of Florida, with a strong presence on Chicago's north and west sides. Allied with Folk Nation gangs and rivals of People Nation gangs including the Latin Kings.

Houthis.description: The Houthis, officially known as Ansar Allah, are a Zaydi revivalist and Shia Islamist political and military organization that emerged from Yemen in the 1990s. Founded by Hussein al-Houthi, the group has been a central player in Yemen's civil war and is backed by Iran. It is designated as a terrorist organization by several countries and is known for human rights abuses including targeting civilians and using child soldiers.

The United Nations reported physical meetings occurring between the two militant groups in February 2025.

edges.evidence_quote: Al Qaeda Arabia Province (AQAP) and Houthis had previously fought, but since 2022 they have had a ceasefire. As part of this ceasefire they have cooperated in attacks against Yemeni government, provided safe havens for each other in their territories, and cooperating in security and intelligence.

descriptions: Hezbollah influenced Houthi ideology and slogan adoption.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2003, influenced by the Lebanese Shia political and military organization Hezbollah, the Houthis adopted their official slogan against the United States, Israel, and the Jews.

descriptions: Houthis were influenced by Hezbollah in adopting their slogan.
edges.evidence_quote: Under the leadership of Zaydi religious leader Hussein al-Houthi, the Houthis emerged as an opposition movement to Yemen president Ali Abdullah Saleh, whom they accused of corruption and being backed by Saudi Arabia and the United States. In 2003, influenced by the Lebanese Shia political and military organization Hezbollah, the Houthis adopted their official slogan against the United States, Israel, and the Jews.

descriptions: Hezbollah's Unit 3800 provides training and strategic assistance to Houthis.
edges.evidence_quote: Since established in 2003, Hezbollah 's Unit 3800 has provided the Houthis training and strategic assistance.

descriptions: Hezbollah commanders actively directed Houthi attacks in 2024.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2024, commanders from IRGC and Hezbollah were reported to be actively involved on the ground in Yemen, overseeing and directing Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping, according to a report by Reuters.

descriptions: Hezbollah influenced the Houthis' ideology and slogan.

descriptions: Iran backs the Houthis as part of the Axis of Resistance.
edges.evidence_quote: The Houthis are backed by Iran, and they are widely considered part of the Iranian-led 'Axis of Resistance'.

descriptions: IRGC provides weapons and training to the Houthis.
edges.evidence_quote: In March 2017, Qasem Soleimani, head of Iran's Quds Force, met with Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) regarding the 'newly accelerated effort to empower the Houthis', including the provision of weapons and training.

descriptions: IRGC commander claimed they directed Houthi attacks on Saudi oil tankers.
edges.evidence_quote: In August 2018, despite previous Iranian denial of military support for the Houthis, IRGC commander Nasser Shabani was quoted by the Iranian Fars News Agency as saying, "We (IRGC) told Yemenis [Houthi rebels] to strike two Saudi oil tankers, and they did it", on 7 August 2018.

descriptions: IRGC commanders actively directed Houthi attacks in 2024.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2024, commanders from IRGC and Hezbollah were reported to be actively involved on the ground in Yemen, overseeing and directing Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping, according to a report by Reuters.

descriptions: North Korea tried to sell weapons to Houthis.
edges.evidence_quote: In August 2018, Reuters reported that a confidential United Nations investigation had found the North Korean government had failed to discontinue its nuclear and missile delivery programs, and in conjunction, was "cooperating militarily with Syria" and was "trying to sell weapons to Yemen's Houthis".

descriptions: North Korea shipped weapons to Houthis via Iran in 2024.
edges.evidence_quote: In January 2024, South Korea's Yonhap News Agency reported that North Korea had evidently shipped weapons to Houthis via Iran, based on the writings in Hangul script that were found on missiles launched towards Israel.

descriptions: Houthis possessed Russian-made missiles transferred via Syria and Iran.
edges.evidence_quote: It was noted by Newsweek in July 2024 that the Houthis were in possession of Russian-made P-800 Oniks missiles, and that the transfer had likely occurred via Syria and Iran.

descriptions: Russian GRU personnel assisted Houthi attacks on merchant ships.
edges.evidence_quote: In August 2024, Middle East Eye, citing a US official, reported that personnel of Russia's GRU were stationed in Houthi-controlled parts of Yemen to assist the militia's attacks on merchant ships.

Orchard Park Trailblazers.description: A street gang based in the Roxbury area of Boston, Massachusetts, founded in the late 1980s. Primarily African American, the gang is involved in drug trafficking, robbery, and murder. It has been active since the 1980s and is known for a long-running feud with the Columbia Point Dawgs. Law enforcement crackdowns occurred in the 1990s and 2000s.

descriptions: Orchard Park Trailblazers are listed as rivals of Columbia Point Dawgs in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Orchard Park Trailblazers

descriptions: The Lenox Street Boys are allied with the Orchard Park Trailblazers.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Orchard Park Trailblazers , Heath Street Gang, Academy Homes Gang, Villa Victoria Park Gang , Everton Geneva Grizzleys

descriptions: Orchard Park Trailblazers and Columbia Point Dawgs are bitter rivals engaged in a long-standing feud.
edges.evidence_quote: They are bitter rivals of the Columbia Point Dawgs . Both gangs have been feuding on the streets of Boston for nearly 30 years.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Columbia Point Dawgs , H Block, Dudley Park, Mt. Pleasant, Ruggles Street

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Columbia Point Dawgs , H Block, Dudley Park, Mt. Pleasant, Ruggles Street

Lueders Park Piru.description: The Lueders Park Piru is a set of the Piru gang alliance, part of the larger Bloods alliance, based in Compton, California. Originating as the Lueders Park Hustlers, it was instrumental in forming the Bloods in 1972. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, robbery, and weapons sales, with approximately 140 members in 2005. It has rivalries with several Crip sets and was allied with Mob Piru before 2009.

descriptions: Kelly Park Compton Crips retaliated against Lueders Park Piru for a shooting, leading to a gang war.
edges.evidence_quote: members of the Kelly Park Compton Crips planned to retaliate by killing a member of the Lueders Park Piru

descriptions: Lueders Park Hustlers helped form the Bloods alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: an independent street gang which was instrumental in the formation of the Bloods gang alliance in 1972.

descriptions: Lueders Park Piru attacked a house used by Butler Blocc Crips.
edges.evidence_quote: The house was located within the territory of the Lueders Park Piru, but was used as a "hang-out" by members of a rival gang known as the Butler Blocc Crips.

descriptions: Kelly Park Compton Crips retaliated against Lueders Park Piru for a killing.
edges.evidence_quote: The Kelly Park Compton Crips held the Lueders Park Piru responsible for the death of Stevens, and in retaliation, killed 30-year-old Don Turner

descriptions: Lueders Park Piru and Mob Piru became enemies after internal conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: the two gangs became enemies after the internal conflict resulting in multiple murders during the Death Row Records era.

descriptions: Lueders Park Piru sided with Mob Piru during a gang war.
edges.evidence_quote: the Lueders Park Piru sided with the Mob Piru

descriptions: Lueders Park Piru is a set within the Piru gang alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The Lueders Park Piru (also known as the Lueders Park Piru Bloods ) are a " set " of the Piru gang alliance

descriptions: A rivalry and conflict developed between Mob Piru and Lueders Park Piru after a murder in 2009.
edges.evidence_quote: a rivalry developed after a Lueders Park Piru member was murdered in April 2009, leading to a series of shootings between 2009 and 2013

descriptions: Mob Piru and Lueders Park Piru were previously one gang before splitting.
edges.evidence_quote: the Mob Piru and Lueders Park Piru were previously one gang, until they split apart

descriptions: Lueders Park Piru is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mob Piru , Fruit Town Piru , Lueders Park Piru , Santana Blocc Compton Crips , Neighbourhood Compton Crips

descriptions: The Errol Flynns are the precursors to JBFL, meaning JBFL succeeded them.
edges.evidence_quote: The Errol Flynns are recognized as the precursors to most, if not all, Detroit gangs that followed in their wake. Some of the most notable successors include the "Be Like Boys", "Dexter Boys" (an offshoot of YBI), "Schoolcraft Boys or SCB's", "SNS", "Fenkell Boys", "FMK" ("Fenkell Mafia Killers"), "7 Mile Killers or 7 Mile Dogs", "Linwood Boys", "Brewster Boys", "Jeffries Boys", and "8 Mile sconys", ("JBFL") "Joy Boys" or "Exit 9"

descriptions: Mad Swan Bloods are listed as allies of Florencia 13 in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Mexican Mafia , Sureños , Pueblo Bishop Bloods , Mad Swan Bloods , Harvard Park Brims , East Coast Crips (Former rivals)

descriptions: The Misso clan was founded together with the Pirozzi and Savarese families.
edges.evidence_quote: La nascita del sodalizio criminale tra le tre famiglie Misso, Pirozzi e Savarese sarebbe da datare intorno agli anni '80
